steps.title: Use the two values as a table
steps.content: Since $f$ is linear, the two values determine the whole function. In Desmos, enter $x_1=[0,1]$, $y_1=[8,12]$, and $y_1\sim mx_1+b$. The regression gives $m=4$ and $b=8$, so $f(x)=4x+8$.

steps.title: Check the shortcut by hand
steps.content: The slope is $\frac{f(1)-f(0)}{1-0}=\frac{12-8}{1}=4$. Since $f(0)=8$, the y-intercept is $8$. That confirms $f(x)=4x+8$.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $f(x)=12x+8$ — slope is 12, but the slope should be 4. Eliminate.
  • B) $f(x)=4x$ — gives $f(0)=0$, not 8. Eliminate.
  • C) $f(x)=4x+12$ — gives $f(0)=12$, not 8. Eliminate.
  • D) $f(x)=4x+8$ — gives $f(0)=8$ and $f(1)=12$. Keep.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
